Risk Description: Because of SLA compliance improvement from 91% to 98% and KPI index improvement from 78 to 90 that was achieved through tightened dependency handling and faster closure routines as documented in Finance & Metrics, but with 7 blocked items, 15 high-priority issues, and continued QA and security pressure still present in the backlog as evidenced across operational and financial data, there is a risk that the improved SLA and KPI performance cannot be sustained through final sprint activities under increased closure pressure, which could lead to SLA degradation below the 94.5% average, missed delivery commitments, customer escalations, potential contract penalty triggers if performance metrics fall below agreed thresholds, undermined stakeholder confidence in delivery capability, potentially triggering enhanced oversight requirements, customer-imposed controls, reputational damage impacting future contract renewals, reference-ability, and the organization's competitive position in the finance domain market.
Tech Stack: SLA Monitoring, Performance Dashboards, JIRA
Potential Impact: SLA degradation below 94.5% average triggering contractual concerns, missed delivery commitments affecting customer trust, customer escalations requiring senior management intervention, potential contract penalty triggers causing financial impact, undermined stakeholder confidence in delivery capability, enhanced oversight requirements increasing management burden, customer-imposed controls restricting operational flexibility, reputational damage affecting market perception, impact on future contract renewals and revenue, reduced reference-ability for new business, and degraded competitive position in the finance domain market.

SLA Metric: Using Angular 18, .NET 8, SQL Server 2019, HTTP 1.1; N-1 versioning strategy
Risk Title: Software Version Upgrade and Technical Debt Accumulation Risk
Risk Description: Because Axos follows an N-1 versioning strategy for all software (currently Angular 18, .NET 8, SQL Server 2019, HTTP 1.1) and upgrades only when necessary as documented in QBR, there is a risk that delayed upgrades will accumulate technical debt and create security or compatibility issues, which could lead to technical debt accumulation affecting long-term maintainability, security vulnerabilities from outdated software versions, compatibility issues with third-party systems and services, increased maintenance costs, and potential upgrade disruption requiring significant effort when upgrades become mandatory.
Tech Stack: Angular 18, .NET 8, SQL Server 2019, Version Management, HTTP 1.1
Potential Impact: Technical debt accumulation significantly affecting long-term system maintainability and increasing future development costs, security vulnerabilities from outdated software versions exposing the system to threats, compatibility issues with evolving third-party systems and services, increased maintenance costs from supporting older versions, and potential major upgrade disruption requiring significant effort and causing business interruption when upgrades become mandatory due to security or compatibility requirements.

SLA Metric: Zero critical/high defects in R0.2 regression
Risk Title: Quality Sustainability Risk
Risk Description: Because Release 0.2 achieved zero critical or high defects in Ascendion regression testing, setting a high-quality bar as documented in QBR, there is a risk that subsequent releases may not maintain this quality level due to schedule pressure or complexity, which could lead to quality degradation in future releases, defect leakage to production, UAT issues, potential production defects, customer dissatisfaction, and reputational damage.
Tech Stack: QA Processes, Quality Management, JIRA
Potential Impact: Quality degradation in future releases failing to maintain established standards, defect leakage to production causing customer-reported issues, UAT issues requiring extended cycles, potential production defects affecting business operations, customer dissatisfaction with quality, and reputational damage affecting market perception and future business opportunities.

SLA Metric: Token invalidation post-logout accepted risk; Tokens valid until expiration
Risk Title: Authentication Security Window Vulnerability Risk
Risk Description: Because token-based authentication has an accepted vulnerability where tokens remain valid until short expiration time even after logout, and this is an accepted risk across multiple projects as documented in QBR, there is a risk that unauthorized access could occur within the token validity window, which could lead to potential unauthorized access to sensitive financial data, security incidents within the validity window, compliance concerns from auditors, limited window of vulnerability, and audit findings requiring remediation or risk acceptance documentation.
Tech Stack: Authentication Framework, Token Management, .NET 8
Potential Impact: Potential unauthorized access to sensitive financial data within token validity window, security incidents occurring during the vulnerability window, compliance concerns raised by auditors regarding authentication controls, limited but exploitable window of vulnerability, and audit findings requiring formal risk acceptance documentation or remediation implementation.

SLA Metric: Project Completion: ~34% (vs 100% expected); Extended timelines to mid-July/August 2026
Risk Title: Critical Schedule Slippage and Delivery Risk
Risk Description: Because of only 34% project completion against Release 1 baseline despite extended timelines to mid-July/August 2026 as documented in MBR, there is a risk that critical milestones will continue to slip, which could lead to continued schedule delays affecting all downstream activities, potential scope descoping to meet deadlines reducing deliverables, customer relationship damage from repeated delays, financial penalties for missed contractual commitments, and overall project viability concerns.
Tech Stack: Project Management Tools
Potential Impact: Continued schedule delays affecting all downstream activities and commitments, potential scope descoping to meet deadlines significantly reducing deliverables and business value, customer relationship damage from repeated delays and missed commitments, substantial financial penalties for missed contractual obligations, and overall project viability concerns potentially leading to contract termination or major restructuring.
